text.title: backup-database-mysql-mongo Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-database-mysql-mongo as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates mysqldump command without --single-transaction for an InnoDB database, causing production write locks during backup window.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include --single-transaction for InnoDB. For mixed-engine databases (InnoDB + MyISAM), add --lock-all-tables explicitly and document the lock window.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates XtraBackup script that uploads the raw --backup output to S3 without running --prepare first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Raw XtraBackup output is crash-consistent only. --prepare applies the redo log to produce a transaction-consistent state needed for restore. Always prepare before upload.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates mongodump without --oplog for a replica set, producing an inconsistent cross-collection snapshot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: --oplog is mandatory for consistent MongoDB backups on replica sets. Without it, a restore will have collections at different timestamps, causing application-level data corruption.
